new_deals_notified.merger_name: MassMutual, Rest, Aware Super and Goodman – Moorabbin Airport

acquirers.name: MAC HoldCo Pty Ltd (an entity which has not been established yet)

new_deals_notified.merger_description: A group of investors proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Moorabbin Airport Corporation Pty Ltd (**MAC**), to be held through a holding company. MAC holds the long-term airport lease granted by the Commonwealth in relation to Moorabbin Airport, a metropolitan airport located south-east of the Melbourne CBD. The investors also propose to acquire leasehold interests in various non-aviation commercial properties located at the Moorabbin Airport site (together, the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Questionnaire - MassMutual, Rest, Aware Super and Goodman - Moorabbin Airport
events.display_title: Questionnaire - MassMutual, Rest, Aware Super and Goodman - Moorabbin Airport

new_deals_notified.merger_description: Zurich Financial Services Australia Limited (**Zurich**) proposes to acquire all of the issued shares in ClearView Wealth Limited (**ClearView**) (the **Acquisition**).
Gilead Sciences, Inc. (Gilead), through its wholly owned subsidiary Ojas Merger Sub LLC, proposes to acquire all of the outstanding shares of Ouro Medicines, LLC (Ouro) (the Acquisition).

acquirers.name: ORIGIN ENERGY INTERNATIONAL HOLDINGS PTY LIMITED

targets.name: CPP Investment Board Private Holdings (4) Inc.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Origin Energy International Holdings Pty Ltd (a wholly owned subsidiary of Origin Energy Limited) (**Origin**) is participating in a series of related transactions which are being effected to achieve the spin off of the Kraken technology business, operated by Kraken Technologies Limited and its related companies (**Kraken**), from Octopus Energy Group Limited (**OEGL**).

events.title: Origin - Equity stake in Kraken - Questionnaire
events.display_title: Origin - Equity stake in Kraken - Questionnaire

events.title: Origin – Equity stake in Kraken - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Origin Energy International Holdings Pty Ltd (a wholly owned
subsidiary of Origin Energy Limited) (Origin) is participating in a
series of related transactions which are being effected to achieve
the spin off of the Kraken technology business, operated by Kraken
Technologies Limited and its related companies (Kraken), from
Octopus Energy Group Limited (OEGL).
Origin will maintain its existing economic interest in Kraken (which is
through Origin’s interest in OEGL) by acquiring Kraken shares from
another existing shareholder and being issued with an additional
equity interest in Kraken in exchange for agreeing to remove and
irrevocably waive its current exclusivity to the Kraken platform in
Australia (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Origin Energy International Holdings Pty Ltd, is a wholly
owned subsidiary of Origin Energy Limited. Origin Energy Limited is
an ASX-listed integrated energy company which, relevantly in this
context, engages in the retail supply of electricity and gas as well as
broadband services in Australia. Origin has an existing indirect
interest in Kraken through its existing direct interest in OEGL. In
substance, the Acquisition maintains Origin’s economic interest in
Kraken and OEGL.
The target, Kraken, is a technology provider, wholly owned by OEGL,
that develops and licenses a proprietary retail and operations
platform, called “the Kraken platform”. The platform is used by utility
providers globally to manage billing, customer service, distributed
energy resources and operational processes.

There is a vertical relationship between Origin’s energy retailing and
broadband services businesses, and Kraken’s consumer relationship
management (CRM) and billing system.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and information from third partes.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• Origin would be unlikely to have the ability and incentive to
foreclose rivals’ access to customer service software services
(including CRM and billing services) or access confidential
information of competitors given there are sufficient alternative
suppliers of these services in Australia and internationally.
• The substantive change to Origin’s interest in Kraken through the
Acquisition is minimal, meaning there is no meaningful increase
to Origin’s ability or incentive to engage in the above conduct.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

deals_cleared.merger_name: Prime Super - Additional interest in Peninsula Link Project

acquirers.name: Southern Way PS Pty Limited as trustee for PS Southern Way Unit Trust

targets.name: Southern Way OSF Pty Limited as trustee for OSF Southern Way Unit Trust

deals_cleared.merger_description: Southern Way PS Pty Limited as trustee for PS Southern Way Unit Trust, which is a wholly owned entity of Prime Super Pty Ltd as trustee for Prime Super (together, **Prime Super**), will increase its percentage interest in Southern Way Holdings Pty Ltd and Southern Way Holdings Trust (collectively referred to as **Southern Way Holdings**) from 18.79% to 27.36% pursuant to its pre-emptive rights in relation to both entities (the **Acquisition**). The Acquisition involves a purchase of stapled units in Southern Way Holding Trust and shares in Southern Way Holdings Pty Limited.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Southern Way PS Pty Limited as trustee for PS Southern Way Unit
Trust, which is a wholly owned entity of Prime Super Pty Ltd as
trustee for Prime Super (together, Prime Super), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of an
additional 8.57% of stapled units and shares in Southern Way
Holdings Pty Ltd and Southern Way Holdings Trust (together,
Southern Way Holdings), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
As a result of the Acquisition, Prime Super will hold a 27.36% interest
in Southern Way Holdings.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
20 April 2026

The acquirer, Prime Super, is a multi-industry fully independent profit-
to-members superannuation fund with approximately $8.4 billion in
funds under management and 140,000 members. Prime Super’s
investment portfolio comprises a number of different assets
including infrastructure investments which represent approximately
16% of its total investment holdings. Those infrastructure
investments include investments in international education
infrastructure funds, energy infrastructure projects, an Australian
based parking group, and an existing 18.79% interest in Southern
Way Holdings. Prime Super does not hold interests in any
infrastructure assets or projects similar to Peninsula Link, nor does it
hold investments in any other entities which provide large scale road
infrastructure services to an Australian state government.
The target, Southern Way Holdings, is a special purpose vehicle
which is responsible for the Peninsula Link Project. The Peninsula
Link Project is a Public Private Partnership to design, construct,
maintain and operate the 27km Peninsula Link Freeway between
Frankston and Mount Martha in Victoria, Australia. The Peninsula
Link Project has a 25-year operation period after which ownership of
the road will be handed back to the State of Victoria (expected in
2038).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
• Based on the information currently before it, including publicly
available information regarding the Peninsula Link Project, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship
between Prime Super’s other investment holdings and
Southern Way Holdings in the supply of large-scale road
infrastructure services
b. the increment to Prime Super’s existing interest in Southern
Way Holdings is small
c. Southern Way Holding’s obligations with respect to the
Peninsula Link Project are governed, and will continue to be
governed post-acquisition, by long-term contracts with the
Victorian state government.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_name: Princeton Digital Group Australia - land located in Campbelltown, NSW

acquirers.name: Princeton Digital Group (Australia Alpha) Prop Pty Ltd

deals_cleared.merger_description: Princeton Digital Group (Australia Alpha) Prop Pty Ltd (**Princeton Digital Group Australia**), as trustee of Princeton Digital Group (Australia Alpha) Prop Trust, proposes to acquire land located in Campbelltown, NSW from MH Property No. 1 Pty Ltd (**MH Property**)(the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Princeton Digital Group (Australia Alpha) Prop Pty Ltd (Princeton
Digital Group Australia) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of land located in Campbelltown, NSW from
MH Property No. 1 Pty Ltd, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
21 April 2026

The acquirer, Princeton Digital Group Australia, is a subsidiary of
Princeton Digital Group. Prince Digital Group is headquartered in
Singapore and operates a data centre business across more than 20
centres in seven countries. Princeton Digital Group does not own or
operate any data centre assets in Australia.
The land located in Campbelltown, NSW is currently vacant and
contains no existing fixtures, structures or buildings. The land will be
acquired by way of a land title transfer from MH Property No.1 Pty
Ltd, which is an Australian property developer.
The Acquisition is for the purpose of building, owning and operating
a data centre in Australia. Princeton Digital Group intends to supply
data centre facilities and associated infrastructure to tenants in
Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABVIn (2)(b) of the
Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Princeton Digital Group does not operate any data centre
facilities in Australia
b. there are other suppliers of data centre facilities and
associated infrastructure in Australia.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-80008/Princeton Digital Group Australia - land located in Campbelltown, NSW - Notification Waiver Determination - 21 April 2026.pdf

deals_cleared.merger_name: Egis Projects Asia Pacific - Fulton Hogan Egis O&M

acquirers.name: EGIS PROJECTS ASIA PACIFIC PTY LIMITED

targets.name: FULTON HOGAN EGIS O&M PTY LTD

deals_cleared.merger_description: Egis Projects Asia Pacific Pty Ltd (**EPAP**) proposes to acquire from Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (**Fulton Hogan Industries**) the 50% of shares in Fulton Hogan Egis O&M Pty Ltd (**FHE**) that it does not already own.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Egis Projects Asia Pacific Pty Limited (EPAP), which is a wholly
owned subsidiary of Egis S.A. (Egis), applied for a notification waiver
in respect of EPAP’s proposed acquisition of the 50% of shares in
Fulton Hogan Egis O&M Pty Ltd (FHE) which it does not already own,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
22 April 2026

The target, FHE, was incorporated in December 2015 as a joint
venture between EPAP and Fulton Hogan Industries Pty Ltd (Fulton
Hogan Industries).
FHE currently supplies operation and maintenance services (i.e.,
asset management, incident response, maintenance, and capital
works to ensure safety, efficiency, and availability) to the
WestConnex project and, in future, will supply facilities management
services for the under-construction M6 tunnel project, also in Sydney.
To date, the supply of facilities management services for the M6
tunnel project is FHE’s only non-WestConnex related business.
The acquirer, EPAP, is a wholly owned subsidiary of Egis S.A., a
France-based engineering, consulting and operation services
company. In Australia, Egis S.A. (via its subsidiaries) provides façade
design and engineering services, building sustainability services,
multi-disciplinary engineering consulting services, architecture
services, and enterprise asset management solutions.
EPAP conducts business development activities relating to
operations and maintenance services supplied by FHE and other
subsidiaries.
The vendor, Fulton Hogan Industries, provides services in pavement,
asphalting, civil construction, and road and asset maintenance
across Australia in sectors including defence, roads, and airports.
Other than through the existing FHE joint venture, neither EPAP nor
any of its connected entities are involved in the supply of operations,
maintenance, and facilities management services for roads and
tunnels in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
• had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Outside the existing FHE joint venture arrangement, there are
no horizontal overlaps between EPAP (including its
connected entities) and FHE in the supply of operations,
maintenance, and facilities management services for roads
and tunnels in Australia.
b. The Acquisition will not result in any reduction in the number
of suppliers of operations, maintenance, and facilities
management services for roads and tunnels in Australia.
c. There are alternative suppliers of operations, maintenance,
and facilities management services for roads and tunnels in
Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-70011/Egis Projects Asia Pacific - Fulton Hogan Egis O_M (WA-70011) - Notification waiver determination - 22 April 2026_0.pdf

acquirers.name: Herman Corp Pty Ltd (as trustee for the Herman Holdings Family Trust)

deals_cleared.merger_description: Winning Appliances is a retailer of kitchen, laundry and other home appliances which it supplies to consumers and businesses across Australia.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Winning Group Holdings Pty Ltd (Winning Holdings) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of:
• Winning Holding’s acquisition of all shares in Winning Appliances
Pty Ltd (Winning Appliances) from the existing Winning
Appliances shareholders (the Shareholders), and
• the Shareholders’ acquisition, in equal proportions to their former
shareholdings in Winning Appliances, of all shares in Winning
Holdings,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
23 April 2026

Winning Appliances supplies and installs kitchen, laundry and other
home appliances including household whitegoods, homewares and
audio-visual products, to consumers and businesses. Winning
Appliances also provides delivery, installation, warehousing and
commercial supply services to builders and developers through its
subsidiaries.
Winning Holdings is a newly established holding company that was
incorporated for the purposes of the Acquisition. It does not
currently provide, nor does it own or have an interest in any business
that provides, goods or services. As such, there is no competitive
overlap between the parties.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal
consolidation, removal of a competitor, or change to Winning
Appliances’ competitive position as a result of the Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC also considered the potential application of section
51ABD of the Act. The parties submit that there are strong grounds
that section 51ABD applies to the Acquisition, however, on the facts
of this matter, including the staged structure of the Acquisition, there
is some uncertainty about whether the Acquisition falls within the
scope of that provision. Having regard to the particular
circumstances, including the consequences of non-notification in
reliance on the exemption if it was found not to apply, the ACCC
considered it appropriate to make a determination on the basis that
the acquisition provisions in the Act apply to the Acquisition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Creative Planning Holdco, LLC (**Creative Planning**), through its wholly owned subsidiary, CP Britannia Bidco Limited, will acquire 100% of the interests in MASECO LLP (**Maseco**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Creative Planning Holdco, LLC (Creative Planning), through its
wholly owned subsidiary, CP Britannia Bidco Limited, applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the interests in MASECO LLP (Maseco), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
22 April 2026

The acquirer, Creative Planning, headquartered in the United States,
is a wealth and investment management firm that provides a
financial planning led investment management approach, retirement
planning, estate planning, trust services, tax planning and family
office services for individuals as well as 401(k) and institutional
clients.
Creative Planning is jointly controlled by Peter Mallouk, TPG Inc.
(TPG) and General Atlantic Service Company, L.P. (GA).
• Peter Mallouk is the President and CEO of Creative Planning.
• TPG is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in
San Francisco in 1992.
• GA is affiliated with a group of investment funds (the GA
Group) that provide capital for growth companies globally.
The GA Group, headquartered in the US, provides capital and
strategic support for growth companies in six sectors:
climate, consumer, financial services, healthcare, life
sciences, and technology.
The target, Maseco is a UK based provider of wealth management
services primarily to high-net worth and ultra-high net worth UK or US
citizens living in the UK or the US. Maseco's presence in Australia is
small.
Both parties provide financial advice and wealth management
globally.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Creative
Planning (and its connected entities) and Maseco in the
supply of financial advice and wealth management services
in Australia
b. the parties’ combined share in the supply of financial advice
and wealth management services in Australia is estimated
to be low and the increase in share of supply arising from
the Acquisition would be small
c. there are alternative suppliers of financial advice and wealth
management services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Star Hotels Group is an independently owned and operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating pubs along the eastern seaboard of Queensland and four venues in South Australia. It currently also operates several pubs in western Queensland, including in Stanthorpe, Westbrook, and Dalby, as well as a retail liquor store in Toowoomba under the Star Liquor brand.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
SGL Hotels Pty Ltd and SCHF Pty Ltd (together, Star Hotels Group)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of the business assets and freehold land of Fiveways
Hotel Toowoomba, as well as two adjacent freehold residential lots,
from MPG Fiveways Prop Pty Ltd and MPG Fiveways Op Pty Ltd, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
23 April 2026

The acquirer, Star Hotels Group, is an independently owned and
operated hospitality group, primarily operating pubs along the
eastern seaboard of Queensland and four venues in South Australia.
It also operates liquor retail outlets under brands including Star
Liquor, Sense of Taste, and Bob’s Bulk Booze, as well as
accommodation at certain venues. In the Toowoomba area, Star
Hotels Group operates Westbrook Tavern (a licensed pub with a
bistro and gaming facilities) and Star Liquor Ruthven Street (a retail
liquor outlet).
The target business, Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba, is currently closed
but previously operated a bar with food and gaming, as well as a
drive-through liquor store. Under the Acquisition, Star Hotels Group
will acquire the freehold land of the business, two adjacent freehold
residential lots, and the business assets (including fixtures, fittings,
and licences) of Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba.
Star Hotels Group and Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba overlap in the
supply of dining, gaming, and liquor services in the Toowoomba
area.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver, and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Star Hotels
Group and Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba in the supply of
dining, gaming and liquor for onsite and offsite consumption
in the Toowoomba area
b. the combined share of the parties in the supply of dining,
gaming, and liquor services in the Toowoomba area is low
c. there are alternative suppliers of dining, gaming, and liquor
services at the local and regional level.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-50013/Star Hotels Group - Fiveways Hotel Toowoomba (WA-50013) - Notification waiver determination - 23 April 2026.pdf

deals_cleared.merger_name: Searchlight Capital Partners - Additional interest in KORE Group Holdings Inc.

acquirers.name: ABRY Partners VII Co-Investment Fund, LP

deals_cleared.merger_description: On 27 February 2026, KONA Parent, L.P. (**KONA Parent**), Kona Merger Sub Co. (**KONA Merger Sub**), a wholly owned subsidiary of KONA Parent, and Kore Group Holdings, Inc. (**KORE**) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger pursuant to which KONA Merger Sub will merge with and into KORE with KORE surviving the merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of KONA Parent (the **Acquisition**). Each share of KORE common stock outstanding at the time of closing will convert to the right to receive $9.25 per share. The Acquisition is valued at an enterprise value of approximately USD$726 million, including debt. As a result of the Acquisition, funds managed by Searchlight Capital Partners (**Searchlight**) will hold an interest of approximately 68.7% in and indirectly acquire sole control of KORE , while funds managed by Abry Partners (**Abry**) will continue to hold a non-controlling minority interest of approximately 21.2%.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
KONA Parent, L.P. (KONA Parent), KONA Merger Sub Co. (KONA
Merger Sub), Searchlight Capital IV LP, ABRY Partners VII, LP, and
ABRY Partners VII Co-Investment Fund, LP applied for a notification
waiver in respect of the proposed merger of KONA Merger Sub with
and into KORE Group Holdings, Inc. (KORE), with KORE surviving the
merger as a wholly owned subsidiary of KONA Parent, as described
in the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).
The Acquisition would result in:
• funds managed by Searchlight Capital Partners (Searchlight)
holding an interest of approximately 68.7% in and indirectly
acquiring sole control of KORE, and
• funds managed by Abry Partners (Abry) continuing to hold a
non-controlling minority interest of approximately 21.2% in
KORE.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
22 April 2026

The acquirer, KONA Parent, was formed solely for the purpose of
entering into the Acquisition. Following the Acquisition, KONA Parent
would be indirectly owned by:
• Searchlight (with an approximate interest of 68.7%), which
manages Searchlight Capital IV LP, and is an investment
firm in both private equity and debt, in sectors such as
communications, media and financial services, operating in
North America and Europe, and
• Abry (with an approximate interest of 21.2%), which
manages ABRY Partners VII, LP and ABRY Partners VII Co-
Investment Fund LP, and is an investment fund focused on
middle market companies in the communications, media,
information and business services industries.
The target, KORE, would be a wholly owned subsidiary of KONA
Parent following the Acquisition. It is a supplier of Internet-of-Things
(IoT) connectivity to the internet and other IoT applications and
solutions based in the US. In Australia, KORE supplies these services
to industries including connected health, industrial IoT, EV charging,
fleet management, smart buildings and sustainability.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the Acquisition is
unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In
particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap between the products and
services supplied by KORE, and Searchlight and Abry
(including their controlled portfolio companies) in Australia,
and there are no material vertical relationships
b. while there may be a small complementary relationship
between the products and services supplied by KORE, and
Searchlight and Abry (including their controlled portfolio
companies), there are alternative suppliers of these products
and services in Australia, and
c. KORE has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_name: Coles – land around supermarket site in Balaclava, VIC
deals_cleared.merger_description: Coles Group Limited (**Coles**) is an Australian public company (ASX: COL) that primarily operates:

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Coles Group Property Developments Limited (CGPD), a property
development arm of Coles Group Limited (Coles), applied for a
notification waiver in respect of CGPD’s proposed acquisition of
parcels of land located at Nelson Street and Alfred Street, adjacent
to the Coles supermarket in Balaclava, Victoria, from the City of Port
Phillip, as described in the transaction documents provided as part
of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
23 April 2026

Coles is an Australian public company (ASX: COL) that primarily
operates:
• supermarkets nationwide, and
• retail liquor stores nationwide, trading under the Liquorland,
Liquorland Warehouse and Liquorland Cellars brands.
Coles has a property development arm, CGPD, that buys land and
constructs shopping centres, standalone supermarkets and/or liquor
outlets and then sells these completed sites to third party buyers.
The vendor, the Port Phillip City Council, is the local government
authority overseeing the City of Port Phillip, an area in inner
Melbourne including the suburbs of Albert Park, Balaclava, Elwood,
part of the Melbourne CBD, Middle Park, part of Port Melbourne,
Ripponlea, South Melbourne, Southbank, St Kilda, St Kilda East,
St Kilda West and Windsor.
The target land proposed for sale includes part of the carpark
adjacent to the Coles supermarket area enclosed by Carlisle, Alfred,
Nelson, and Camden Streets, and some laneways that will be
discontinued.
The freehold interest would be an input to Coles’ supermarket
retailing operations.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided by the applicant and had regard to the
factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act
2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. Coles currently owns the land on which its supermarket in
Balaclava is located and the adjacent land that is the subject
of the Acquisition is unlikely to be able to accommodate a
new supermarket
b. the land that is proposed to be acquired will be used to
improve the quality of the offering of Coles’ existing
supermarket in Balaclava
c. there are several alternative grocery retailers, including
Woolworths, ALDI and IGA, and retail suppliers of packaged
liquor for offsite consumption, in and around the local area.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds determined under section 51ABP(1) being met. The ACCC
does however observe that the targeted notification requirements for
supermarkets determined under section 51ABQ(1) are likely to apply.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

events.url_gh: /mergers/WA-60012/Coles - land around supermarket site in Balaclava, VIC (WA-60012) - Notification waiver determination - 23 April 2026.pdf

deals_cleared.merger_description: Wipro Networks Pte Ltd, a wholly owned subsidiary of Wipro Limited (**Wipro**), proposes to acquire all the shares in Mindsprint Pte Limited (**Mindsprint**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Wipro Networks Pte Ltd, a subsidiary of Wipro Limited (Wipro),
applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Mindsprint Pte Limited
(Mindsprint), as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition does not include Wipro acquiring Mindsprint's
current 51% interest in Mindsprint Pty Ltd (Mindsprint Australia), a
joint venture with XL Tech in Australia.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 April 2026

Wipro is an Indian global technology, consulting and outsourcing
company, with over 230,000 employees serving more than 1,400
clients across 175 cities. Wipro maintains relationships across key
sectors including financial services, healthcare, government, retail,
consumer goods, technology, manufacturing, energy and utilities. Its
business is structured around four global business lines: Technology
Services, Business Process Services, Consulting Services and
Engineering.
Mindsprint is a Singapore-based technology consultancy that offers
information technology and related services for the food & agritech,
manufacturing, retail and consumer packaged goods. It offers
solutions in consulting, enterprise applications, digital engineering,
data analytics, cloud and infrastructure services, cybersecurity,
intelligent enterprise operations and business process outsourcing.
Both Wipro and Mindsprint offer information technology solutions
(including cloud and cybersecurity), business and consulting
services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
• a. Wipro is not acquiring Mindsprint’s interest in Mindsprint
Australia and, beyond the joint venture, Mindsprint has a
small presence in Australia
b. there are alternative providers of information technology,
business and consulting services in Australia, and the
combined share in the supply of these services is estimated
to be low.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Eagers operates 12 dealerships in the Melbourne area, including in Toorak (Mercedes-Benz), Malvern (ULR Jaguar and ULR Land Rover) and Port Melbourne (Jaguar, Volvo, Volkswagen and Land Rover). Eagers does not currently have any Audi dealerships in Victoria.
